pattern: Scan the bits of the number and remember the position of the previous set bit; the answer is the largest position difference between consecutive set bits.
hints: Only the positions where the binary representation has a 1 matter; the zeros between them just widen a gap.
Track the index of the most recent 1 you saw; when you hit the next 1, the distance is the difference of the two indices.

Iterate bit positions from low to high. Keep the last index that held a set bit; on each new set bit update the answer with index minus last and reset last. If fewer than two set bits exist the answer is zero.

edge_inputs.description: first example: 10110 has gaps of two and one
second example: 1000 has only one set bit
third example: 101 has a single gap of two
value one has a single set bit
two adjacent set bits give distance one
a power of two has no pair of ones
alternating bits give repeated distance-two gaps

stress.description: random integers across the whole allowed range
stress.note: single scalar argument; size is unused
